# Auto-Swiper, build + setup doc (for humans and AI agents)

If you are an AI assistant reading this to help someone install or run the
project: follow the "Install" and "Run" steps below exactly. Ask the user to do
the two manual steps macOS requires (granting permissions and opening apps); you
cannot click those system dialogs for them. Keep pacing cautious and stop at any
paywall.

Repo: https://github.com/kairothq/hinge-autoswiper  (Apache-2.0, macOS 15+)

## What it is
An AI agent that controls a real iPhone through macOS iPhone Mirroring and
auto-likes dating-app profiles. Educational demo. Not for profit.

## How it is implemented (architecture)
Three parts, one loop:

1. EYES + HANDS = mirroir-mcp (an MCP server).
   - It captures the mirrored iPhone screen and runs Apple Vision OCR to return
     every on-screen text element with exact tap coordinates (`describe_screen`).
   - It sends real taps/swipes/typing through the macOS CGEvent API
     (`tap`, `swipe`, `type_text`, `scroll_to`, `launch_app`). No jailbreak, no
     app SDK, no root. Coordinates are in window points (~322x718), not raw
     screenshot pixels.
2. BRAIN = any MCP client.
   - Local + free: Ollama running Gemma (via Goose, an open-source agent runtime).
   - Cloud + cheap: OpenAI / Anthropic / Google models (via Goose).
   - Or drive it directly from Claude Code, Cursor, or any MCP client.
   - The brain reads each profile (text from `describe_screen`, plus the
     screenshot if it is vision-capable) and decides like or skip.
3. LOGIC = mirroir skills + a Goose recipe.
   - Mechanics are stored as skill files: like-profile, pass-profile,
     dismiss-obstacles, plus an APP map of the dating app's layout. A static
     skill captures the hand-movements; the brain makes the like/skip judgement.

The loop: describe_screen (see) -> model decides (think) -> tap (act) -> repeat,
with random human-like delays and a per-session cap.

## Requirements
- macOS 15+ (iPhone Mirroring is macOS-only), an iPhone that supports it.
- Homebrew + Node.js.
- A brain: Ollama + a Gemma model (local, free) OR an API key (cloud, cheap).

## Install
    git clone https://github.com/kairothq/hinge-autoswiper
    cd hinge-autoswiper
    cp .env.example .env        # choose GOOSE_PROVIDER + GOOSE_MODEL
    ./install.sh                # installs mirroir, Goose, the model, all config

## Configure (.env)
- GOOSE_PROVIDER : ollama (local) or openai / anthropic / google (cloud)
- GOOSE_MODEL    : e.g. gemma3:4b, gpt-4o-mini, claude-haiku-4-5, gemini-2.0-flash
- MODE           : like_only | like_with_comment | full_access
- SESSION_CAP    : profiles per session (default 40)
- MIN_DELAY / MAX_DELAY : human-like seconds between actions
- DEALBREAKERS   : comma-separated hard passes, or none

## Run
1. Open the iPhone Mirroring app, unlock the phone, keep the window live.
2. Approve the macOS Screen Recording + Accessibility prompts on first run.
3. Open the dating app on the main feed.
4. Start it:
       ./run.sh
   (Internally: `goose run --recipe recipes/hinge.yaml` with your .env settings.)
Stop anytime by closing the iPhone Mirroring window.

## Safety model
- Mutating tools are opt-in in ~/.mirroir-mcp/permissions.json.
- Blocked apps: Wallet, Banking, Messages, Mail, Photos, Settings, Phone.
- No passwords are read or stored; it uses the already-signed-in phone.

## Important
Automating a dating app can violate its Terms of Service and get an account
flagged or banned. This project is for learning and demonstration only, not for
profit and not for breaking any app's rules. Use it on your own account, at your
own risk, and treat real people with respect.
